script_text: Good readers are a little like detectives.
When a sentence does not make sense, they look for clues.
Then they try the word again. Let us see how!
Here are three steps for when a word does not make sense.
Step two. Think. Does that word fit the rest of the sentence?
Step three. Try again with a word that makes sense.
Listen. You read, the cat sat on the mat.
But you said, the cat sat on the map. Uh oh!
Does a cat sit on a map? Stop. Think. That does not make sense.
Try again. The cat sat on the mat. That makes sense!
After every sentence, ask yourself, did that make sense?
Now you are ready to practice using clues to read!
